claims.claim_text: The CKA is an online, proctored, performance-based exam that requires solving multiple tasks from a command line running Kubernetes within 2 hours.
claims.span_hint: line 41 | section: Why This Module Matters; line 473 | section: Did You Know, item 1
claims.rationale: The official CKA certification page directly describes the exam as online, proctored, performance-based, command-line based, and 2 hours long.

claims.claim_text: The current public passing score for the CKA is 66%.
claims.span_hint: line 474 | section: Did You Know, item 2
claims.rationale: The Linux Foundation certification FAQ explicitly states that a score of 66% or above is required to pass the CKA.

claims.claim_text: CKA candidates are allowed to use the Kubernetes documentation site and its built-in search during the exam.
claims.span_hint: line 39 | section: Why This Module Matters; line 216 | section: Part 4; line 703 | section: Sources list
claims.rationale: The official resources-allowed page states that CKA candidates may use kubernetes.io/docs and may use its on-site search as long as they do not open external search results.

claims.claim_text: A kubeconfig context combines cluster, user, and namespace settings, and kubectl uses the current context by default.
claims.span_hint: line 264 | section: Design A Question-Start Routine For Context, Namespace, And Output Files
claims.rationale: The kubeconfig documentation explains that a context contains cluster, namespace, and user information and that kubectl uses the current context by default.

claims.claim_text: Namespaces scope object names, so the same object name in `default` is distinct from the same name in another namespace.
claims.span_hint: line 286 | section: Design A Question-Start Routine For Context, Namespace, And Output Files
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes namespaces documentation defines namespaces as scopes for names and shows that namespaced resources are separated by namespace.

further_reading.title: Linux Foundation CKA FAQ
further_reading.why_relevant: Best primary source for the current passing score, exam duration, and current exam-environment version.

further_reading.title: Resources Allowed for CKA and CKAD
further_reading.why_relevant: This is the authoritative policy for what documentation and tools candidates may access during the exam.

further_reading.title: Organizing Cluster Access Using kubeconfig Files
further_reading.why_relevant: It directly supports the module's question-start routine around contexts, clusters, and namespace defaults.

notes: No shared citation pool exists yet for `k8s/cka/part0-environment`, so `section_pool_ref` is null and `source_ids` remain empty. I skipped analogies, quiz content, exercise steps, code blocks, and the heuristic timing tables as pedagogical scaffolding rather than citation-mandated hard claims; Did You Know item 4 reads as advisory rather than a discrete sourceable fact.
